subject: UX & Research
title: The Twitter Character Limit Wars
description: A quick win: understand The Twitter Character Limit Wars.

hook.text: When Twitter doubled from 140 to 280 characters in 2017, longtime users predicted the death of the platform.

buildup.text: The 140-character limit was Twitter's soul. It forced brevity and creativity. Expanding felt like betrayal.

discovery.text: Studies showed most tweets still stayed under 140. Users had internalized the constraint as part of the product.

twist.text: The panic faded. Twitter survived. But the lesson remained: changing core constraints changes product identity.

climax.text: Constraints define products. Removing them changes what the product means to users, even if they adapt.

punchline.text: Constraints shape identity. Change them carefully.

quiz.question: What did Twitter's character limit expansion reveal?
options: Core constraints become part of a product's identity and changing them is culturally significant
Users always want more space to write
